programmes.show_description: House Calls: Detective drama series. Two horrific murders for Frost to solve make as many demands on the man's compassion as on his professional expertise.
Kids' Outing: Enduring '70s sitcom. Blakey takes pleasure in making Stan and Jack take a school party on an outing, and he also accuses Stan of giving his nephew a girlie magazine.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Bonjour Paris: Classic British sitcom. Oliver and Simon are off to Paris, but they are soon in trouble with a local restaurant, the gendarmerie, and an embassy official.
The Other Woman: '70s sitcom about a bus company. Olive is furious when Arthur flirts with a brassy-looking conductress at a busmen's darts match and then fails to come home.
Bob gets information about Viv from a witness about a specific date his wife was seen with Freddie. Jimmy confronts Lexi, and Eli damages a delivery for Matthew.
David fobs Audrey off when she confronts him about Gail's fall, then turns to a stunned Tina to back him up. Fiz returns from her holiday and Michelle says goodbye to Ryan.
The police quiz David about his past and take Tina in for questioning. Paul looks on enviously as Leanne and Dan make a date and Fiz visits Chesney at the foster home.
True Confessions: When two rich brothers fall under suspicion of murder, Frost's quest for the truth leads to him being suspended. A new lady friend enters his life.
School of Hard Knocks: When an ex-Army schoolteacher takes his strict discipline too far, Joe must race to find a missing gun and a schoolboy bent on revenge against the tutor.
The Runaways: A gangster's accountant is on the run from his underworld bosses, and David finds an old jeep with a surprising history.
Murder on Saint Malley's Day: Feature-length drama. The activities of an elite club at a college which dominates the economy and social life of Midsomer Parva lead to murder.
Carl realises he's been double-crossed when he attends Matthew's presentation. Diane is unsure of Pollard's intentions about the wedding, and Zak pulls out all the stops at work.
Zak quits his job when Matthew reveals he will pay Belle's school fees but only after the insurance pays out. Lexi feels guilty about her actions, and Diane rumbles Eric's scheme.
Leanne considers taking drastic measures to resolve her problems with the restaurant. Tina and David struggle with the burden of their deceit and Fiz meets Chesney's social worker.
Dan is incredulous when Leanne reveals her plans and says she'll get Paul to do her dirty work. Fiz moves into No 5 and the hospital visit leaves David and Tina feeling uneasy.
Cashing In: A flood of forged notes leads the police to believe that a missing gangster is around, and Bernie is over the moon when his favourite celebrity moves in down the road.
A Whiter Shade of Pale: The village simpleton is accused of murdering a local woman, but things change when the murder weapon is found and Joe's professional judgement is questioned.
Cold River: Crime drama. DCI Vera Stanhope investigates when a beauty empire is shaken to the ground after one of three sisters is found dead in the River Tyne.
Uniform: Crime drama series. A crime wave has taken hold of Oxford - a homeless man is grievously assaulted, a young PC is found dead, and a notable artist reported missing.
True Confessions: Detective drama series. When two rich and outwardly successful brothers fall under suspicion of murder, Frost's quest for the truth leads to him being suspended.
The Canteen Girl: Enduring '70s British sitcom. Stan and Jack tease Inspector Blake when they are amused at the attention showered on new canteen girl Molly by their superior.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
X Marks the Spot: Classic British sitcom. Oliver and Simon both decide to stand for election to the local council.
Dangerous Driving: Enduring '70s British sitcom. The busmen decide to get fit after reading an article which states that drivers have a shorter life expectancy than conductors.
Carl realises he's been double-crossed when he attends Matthew's presentation. Diane is unsure of Pollard's intentions about the wedding, and Zak pulls out all the stops at work.
Zak quits his job when Matthew reveals he will pay Belle's school fees but only after the insurance pays out. Lexi feels guilty about her actions, and Diane rumbles Eric's scheme.
Leanne considers taking drastic measures to resolve her problems with the restaurant. Tina and David struggle with the burden of their deceit and Fiz meets Chesney's social worker.
Dan is incredulous when Leanne reveals her plans and says she'll get Paul to do her dirty work. Fiz moves into No 5 and the hospital visit leaves David and Tina feeling uneasy.
No Other Love: Detective drama series. Frost investigates two domestic cases - one tragically straightforward and the other cruelly deceptive - which are solved at a terrible cost.
Cashing In: A flood of forged notes leads the police to believe that a missing gangster is around, and Bernie is over the moon when his favourite celebrity moves in down the road.
A Whiter Shade of Pale: The village simpleton is accused of murdering a local woman, but things change when the murder weapon is found and Joe's professional judgement is questioned.
A Talent for Life: Feature-length drama. Barnaby and Troy have to solve a double murder when a feisty former socialite and a Casanova are found bludgeoned to death.
In the 5000th episode of the soap, Carl realises it was Lexi who double-crossed him. Zak is encouraged by Belle to tell Lisa he has quit his job, and Pollard chooses his best man.
Lisa struggles to cope at work but is surprised by Pollard's compassion when he gives her some money for lunch. Val's daughter returns, and Jake celebrates his 18th birthday.
Fiz despairs of Kirk and tells Maria he will have to move out. David assures Gail he will look after her when she's discharged and Roy feels the strain of having Becky as a lodger.
Gail returns home from hospital and Tina feels the pressure when a suspicious Audrey quizzes her about her alibi. Fiz and Maria struggle to resolve the dilemma over Kirk.
The War of the Roses: Mason and Miller tackle prejudice after an attempt is made on a local farmer's life, and David is terrified that Peggy's double dealings have angered a ghost.
Ties That Bind: Aidensfield is targeted by violent robbers, Mason and Rachel's romantic past puts their careers in jeopardy, and a familiar friend bids farewell.
The Holly Bears a Prickle: Comedy-drama series. Martin and Louisa go out on their first date, but the clumsy doctor cannot help but ruin the occasion with an inappropriate remark.
Nowt So Queer: Comedy-drama series. The village is buzzing with news of the engagement of Martin and Louisa, and Al decides to take action to save his dad Bert's restaurant business.
Exeunt: The last-ever episode of the crime drama series. When a number of cryptic death notices appear in the Oxford Mail, Endeavour is determined to solve the puzzle behind them.
